meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: Solve Debugging Bottlenecks for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with complex debugging. Use our Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship faster, impress clients, and automate your workflow.
content.answer_block: The 'can't debug code' loop ends by shifting from manual tracing to AI-assisted architectural analysis. By deploying the Antigravity project template, you standardize your environment, allowing Claude Code to map dependencies and identify root causes in seconds, effectively turning hours of frustration into a repeatable, automated diagnostic process.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your client’s deadline is looming, and your codebase has become a labyrinth of silent failures. You’re staring at a stack trace that refuses to speak, feeling the weight of time slipping away while your reputation hangs in the balance. We’ve all been there—trapped in a debugging loop where every fix breaks something else.

But the era of manual, line-by-line hair-pulling is over. By integrating the Antigravity project template with the raw reasoning power of Claude Code, you can treat your codebase as a system to be queried rather than a puzzle to be solved. This is how elite freelancers scale their output without sacrificing their sanity.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Antigravity Environment
steps.description: Initialize your project with the Antigravity structure to ensure consistent dependency injection and logging patterns that AI agents can parse easily.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard structure with strict TypeScript enforcement and centralized error logging.

steps.title: Index the Codebase for Context
steps.description: Before debugging, you must provide the AI with a semantic map of your project dependencies and architectural boundaries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the current directory and generate a dependency graph identifying the primary execution paths and state management entry points.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Vector
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a differential analysis between your last stable commit and the current failing state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a diff analysis on the recent changes. Identify which specific module is violating the Antigravity contract protocols.

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Apply the fix while ensuring the surrounding Antigravity boilerplate remains intact, preventing regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a patch to resolve the identified null-reference error in the service layer, maintaining compatibility with the current Antigravity middleware.

steps.title: Verify and Validate
steps.description: Run the integrated test suite to ensure the fix holds under load and satisfies the original client requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the full test suite and verify that the Antigravity heartbeat signals are active and stable across all microservices.

pro_tips: Always keep a 'debug-scratchpad.md' in your root; Claude Code treats this as a persistent memory bank for your session-specific findings.
Use Antigravity's custom logging middleware to output JSON-formatted logs; Claude Code parses structured logs 40% faster than raw text.
When a bug seems impossible, ask Claude to 'simulate a malicious actor'—it often reveals edge-case security vulnerabilities disguised as bugs.
Alias your most frequent Claude Code CLI commands to 2-letter shortcuts; speed is the primary factor in maintaining your flow state.
Never commit a fix without running an 'Antigravity contract check' to ensure your changes didn't drift from the initial project architecture.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is stable, begin refactoring your legacy modules into Antigravity components one by one to ensure future-proof debugging.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. With the right architecture and an AI-first workflow, you can handle 3x the client load with half the effort. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
